message:
test(raptorq): add structured failure contexts, replay refs, and E2E report contract

Systematically upgrade RaptorQ test assertions with deterministic
scenario identification, replay references, and structured logging
fields to support the D5/D7/D9 traceability requirements.

src/raptorq/tests.rs (edge_cases module):
- Add failure_context() helper emitting scenario_id, seed, k,
  symbol_size, and replay_ref on every assertion failure
- Add encoder_with_seed_fallback() + SelectedLargeProfile for robust
  large-block test construction that tries multiple (k, seed) combos
  rather than hard-coding a single potentially-singular configuration
- Convert all .expect() calls to .unwrap_or_else() with structured
  context strings (scenario_id + replay_ref)
- insufficient_symbols_error: validate the actual received/required
  counts in the error payload, not just the variant discriminant
- symbol_size_mismatch_error: validate expected/actual sizes in error
- Each test now carries a canonical replay_ref ID for catalog linkage

tests/raptorq_conformance.rs (pipeline_e2e module):
- Extend Scenario struct with scenario_id, replay_id, profile,
  unit_sentinel, and assertion_id fields
- Extend Report struct with schema_version ("raptorq-e2e-log-v1"),
  run_id, repro_command, and phase_markers array
- Add assert_report_contract() validating all structured fields in the
  serialized JSON report (schema version, scenario/replay IDs, profile
  marker validity, unit sentinel, assertion ID, run ID, repro command,
  phase markers array length)
- Reduce burst_loss_late drop_per_mille from 250 to 150 to stabilize
  the forensics-profile scenario
- All four E2E scenarios now emit full structured metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
